subject: Competia #176 — The economics of fictional companions
snippet: Why the fictional-boyfriend market just hit $1.2B...
bodyHtml:
Competia #176

This week I'm watching the economics of fictional companions. Replika reported $1.2B ARR last quarter, up 47% YoY. Character.ai now claims 28M monthly actives.

The MIT Senseable City Lab released a study (April 14, 2026) showing that 67% of regular companion-app users feel more lonely after sustained use, not less.

Three signals converge: Replika Q1 report, MIT study, and the rise of punk health subcultures that explicitly reject digital companionship.

We are witnessing the end of polite consumption — robustness is the new wellness.

Read the full essay
